1	/**
2	 * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
3	 *
4	 * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
5	 * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
6	 * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
7	 *
8	 * Routes:
9	 *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
10	 *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
11	 *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
12	 *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
13	 */
14	
15	export interface Env {
16	  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
17	}
18	
19	const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
20	const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
21	const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
22	
23	interface ApodResponse {
24	  date: string;
25	  title: string;
26	  explanation: string;
27	  url: string;
28	  hdurl?: string;
29	  media_type: "image" | "video";
30	  copyright?: string;
31	}
32	
33	class NasaApiError extends Error {
34	  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
35	    super(message);
36	  }
37	}
38	
39	function apodCacheUrl(key: string): string {
40	  return `http://apod-cache/${key}`;
41	}
42	
43	async function fetchApod(log: string[], apiKey: string, date?: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
44	  const cacheKey = date ?? "today";
45	
46	  const cache = caches.default;
47	  const cacheReq = new Request(apodCacheUrl(cacheKey));
48	  const cached = await cache.match(cacheReq);
49	  if (cached) {
50	    log.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → cache HIT`);
51	    return cached.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
52	  }
53	
54	  const url = date
55	    ? `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`
56	    : `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}`;
57	
58	  const res = await fetch(url);
59	
60	  const cfCache = res.headers.get("cf-cache-status") ?? "none";
61	  log.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → ${res.status} (cf-cache: ${cfCache})`);
62	
63	  if (!res.ok) {
64	    const body = await res.text();
65	    log.push(`  [fetchApod] body=${body}`);
66	    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
67	      res.status === 429
68	        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
69	        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
70	    );
71	  }
72	
73	  const apod = await res.json() as ApodResponse;
74	  await cache.put(cacheReq, new Response(JSON.stringify(apod), {
75	    headers: { "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}` },
76	  }));
77	  return apod;
78	}
79	
80	async function findLatestImage(log: string[], apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
81	  let videoStreak = 0;
82	
83	  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
84	    const date = i === 0
85	      ? undefined
86	      : (() => { const d = new Date(); d.setDate(d.getDate() - i); return d.toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" }); })();
87	
88	    const apod = await fetchApod(log, apiKey, date);
89	    if (apod.media_type === "image") return apod;
90	    videoStreak++;
91	  }
92	
93	  throw new Error(
94	    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
95	  );
96	}
97	
98	async function fetchImageCached(log: string[], url: string): Promise<Response> {
99	  const cache = caches.default;
100	  const cacheReq = new Request(url);
101	  const cached = await cache.match(cacheReq);
102	  if (cached) {
103	    log.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → cache HIT`);
104	    return cached;
105	  }
106	
107	  const res = await fetch(url);
108	
109	  log.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → ${res.status}`);
110	
111	  if (!res.ok) {
112	    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
113	  }
114	
115	  const cloned = res.clone();
116	  await cache.put(cacheReq, new Response(cloned.body, {
117	    headers: { ...Object.fromEntries(res.headers), "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}` },
118	  }));
119	  return res;
120	}
121	
122	function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
123	  return {
124	    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
125	    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
126	    "Link": [
127	      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
128	      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
129	      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
130	      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
131	    ].join(", "),
132	    ...headers,
133	  };
134	}
135	
136	export default {
137	  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
138	    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
139	      return new Response(null, {
140	        headers: {
141	          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
142	          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
143	          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
144	        },
145	      });
146	    }
147	
148	    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
149	    const { pathname } = reqUrl;
150	    const origin = reqUrl.origin;
151	    const start = Date.now();
152	
153	    const log: string[] = [];
154	    let res: Response;
155	
156	    try {
157	      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
158	
159	      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd") {
160	        const apod = await findLatestImage(log, apiKey);
161	        const imageUrl = pathname === "/sd"
162	          ? apod.url
163	          : (apod.hdurl || apod.url);
164	
165	        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(log, imageUrl);
166	        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
167	
168	        res = new Response(upstream.body, {
169	          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
170	        });
171	      } else if (pathname === "/info") {
172	        const endpoints = {
173	          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
174	          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
175	          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
176	          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
177	        };
178	
179	        try {
180	          const apod = await fetchApod(log, apiKey);
181	
182	          res = new Response(
183	            JSON.stringify({
184	              title: apod.title,
185	              date: apod.date,
186	              explanation: apod.explanation,
187	              media_type: apod.media_type,
188	              url: apod.url,
189	              hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
190	              copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
191	              endpoints,
192	            }, null, 2),
193	            { headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
194	          );
195	        } catch (err) {
196	          const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
197	          const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
198	          res = new Response(
199	            JSON.stringify({ error: message, endpoints }, null, 2),
200	            { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
201	          );
202	        }
203	      } else if (pathname === "/about") {
204	        const text = [
205	          "",
206	          "     .        *          .          *          .        *",
207	          "   .         *                        .          .",
208	          "                 _   _    _    ____    _",
209	          "     *          | \\ | |  / \\  / ___|  / \\         .",
210	          "        .       |  \\| | / _ \\ \\___ \\ / _ \\",
211	          "           .    | |\\  |/ ___ \\ ___) / ___ \\   *",
212	          "     .          |_| \\_/_/   \\_\\____/_/   \\_\\        .",
213	          "  *                              .                *",
214	          "        .    *        .    *          .        .",
215	          "",
216	          "                   ── APOD Proxy ──",
217	          "",
218	          "  Proxy for NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.",
219	          "  Returns today's image at a static URL, no API key required.",
220	          "  Video days are skipped automatically.",
221	          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",
222	          "",
223	          "  Usage:",
224	          `    <img src="${origin}/">`,
225	          "",
226	          "  Routes:",
227	          "    /        HD image (default)",
228	          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
229	          "    /info    JSON metadata",
230	          "    /about   This page",
231	          "",
232	          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
233	          "",
234	        ].join("\n");
235	
236	        res = new Response(text, {
237	          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
238	        });
239	      } else {
240	        res = new Response(
241	          JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /info, /about" }),
242	          { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
243	        );
244	      }
245	    } catch (err) {
246	      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
247	      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
248	      res = new Response(
249	        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
250	        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
251	      );
252	    }
253	
254	    console.log(`────────────────────────────────────────`);
255	    console.log(`${request.method} ${pathname} → ${res.status} (${Date.now() - start}ms)`);
256	    for (const line of log) console.log(line);
257	    return res;
258	  },
259	};
260
```

332	      *, *::before, *::after {
333	        animation-duration: 0.01ms;
334	        animation-delay: 0ms;
335	        transition-duration: 0.01ms;
336	      }
337	      .hero-img { opacity: 1; }
338	      .site-header, .hero-tagline, .usage,
339	      .hero-caption, .fade-up {
340	        opacity: 1;
341	        transform: none;
342	      }
343	    }
344	
345	    /* ── MOBILE ── */
346	    @media (max-width: 639px) {
347	      .footer-inner { flex-direction: column; text-align: center; }
348	      .hero-caption { flex-direction: column; gap: 0.15rem; }
349	    }
350	  </style>
351	</head>

353	<body>
354	  <header class="site-header">
355	    <a href="/" class="logo">NASApicture<span class="logo-dot">.</span>com</a>
356	    <h1 class="hero-tagline">Today's Astronomy Picture of the Day</h1>
357	    <nav class="site-nav" aria-label="Site navigation">
358	      <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/about" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">/about</a>

366	        alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day"
367	        class="hero-img"
368	        id="hero-img"
369	        loading="eager"
370	        fetchpriority="high"
371	      >
372	      <div class="hero-caption visible" id="hero-caption">
373	        <span class="hero-caption-title" id="caption-title">10 Years of Detections of Compact Binary Coalescences</span>
374	        <span class="hero-caption-date" id="caption-date">2026-03-26</span>
375	      </div>
376	    </div>
377	
378	    <div class="usage-row">
379	      <span class="usage-item">New day, new photo, same URL <span class="usage-url">https://api.nasapicture.com<button class="copy-btn" data-copy="https://api.nasapicture.com/" aria-label="Copy URL"><svg class="copy" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2"/><path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"/></svg><svg class="check" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg></button></span></span>
380	      <span class="usage-item">Use it anywhere like an image <span class="usage-url">&lt;img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/"&gt;<button class="copy-btn" data-copy='&lt;img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/"&gt;' aria-label="Copy img tag"><svg class="copy" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2"/><path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"/></svg><svg class="check" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg></button></span></span>
381	    </div>

389	          <span class="endpoint-route">/</span>
390	          <span class="endpoint-desc">HD image, falls back to standard</span>
391	          <span class="endpoint-type">image/*</span>
392	        </a>
393	        <a class="endpoint-row" href="https://api.nasapicture.com/sd" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
394	          <span class="endpoint-route">/sd</span>
395	          <span class="endpoint-desc">Standard resolution image</span>
396	          <span class="endpoint-type">image/*</span>
397	        </a>

399	          <span class="endpoint-route">/info</span>
400	          <span class="endpoint-desc">Title, date, explanation, URLs, copyright</span>
401	          <span class="endpoint-type">application/json</span>
402	        </a>
403	        <a class="endpoint-row" href="https://api.nasapicture.com/about" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
404	          <span class="endpoint-route">/about</span>
405	          <span class="endpoint-desc">Description and route listing</span>
406	          <span class="endpoint-type">text/plain</span>
407	        </a>
408	      </div>
409	    </div>
410	  </div>
411	
412	  <footer>
413	    <div class="footer-inner">
414	      <p>Built by <a href="https://grantkeller.dev" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Grant Keller</a></p>
415	      <p>
416	        <a href="https://apod.nasa.gov/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">NASA APOD</a>

419	      </p>

423	  <script>
424	    // Image load
425	    const img = document.getElementById('hero-img');
426	
427	    function onImageReady() { img.classList.add('loaded'); }
428	
429	    if (img.complete && img.naturalWidth > 0) {
430	      onImageReady();
431	    } else {
432	      img.addEventListener('load', onImageReady);
433	    }
434	
435	    // Fetch APOD metadata for caption
436	    fetch('https://api.nasapicture.com/info')
437	      .then(r => r.json())
438	      .then(data => {
439	        if (data.title) {
440	          document.getElementById('caption-title').textContent = data.title;
441	          document.getElementById('caption-date').textContent = data.date;
442	          document.getElementById('hero-caption').classList.add('visible');
443	        }
444	      })
445	      .catch(() => {});
446	
447	    // Copy buttons
448	    document.querySelectorAll('.copy-btn').forEach(btn => {
449	      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
450	        const text = btn.dataset.copy;
451	        navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
452	          btn.classList.add('copied');
453	          setTimeout(() => btn.classList.remove('copied'), 2000);
454	        });
455	      });
456	    });
457	
458	    // Scroll fade-in
459	    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
460	      entries.forEach(entry => {
461	        if (entry.isIntersecting) {
462	          entry.target.classList.add('visible');
463	          observer.unobserve(entry.target);
464	        }
465	      });
466	    }, { threshold: 0.15 });
467	
468	    document.querySelectorAll('.fade-up').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));
469	  </script>
470	</body>
471	</html>
472

 `./README.md`

```text
1	# NASA Picture Proxy
2	
3	A Cloudflare Worker that proxies [NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day](https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html) behind a fixed URL. An HTTP GET request returns today's image without needing an API key or parsing JSON.
4	
5	During a tinkering session, I wanted to set the background image of my homelab [homepage](https://github.com/gethomepage/homepage) dashboard to the APOD. I didn't want to inject additional JavaScript on the page and couldn't find an existing static URL. So, I built this proxy to share with anyone else who'd like to view the wonders of space easily, anywhere.
```
</details></tool-use>

361	  <header class="site-header">

363	    <h1 class="hero-tagline">Today's Astronomy Picture of the Day</h1>
364	    <nav class="site-nav" aria-label="Site navigation">

366	    </nav>
367	  </header>
368	
369	  <section class="hero">
370	    <div class="hero-image-area">
371	      <img
372	        src=""
373	        alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day"
374	        class="hero-img"
375	        id="hero-img"
376	        loading="eager"
377	        fetchpriority="high"
378	      >
379	      <figcaption class="hero-caption" id="hero-caption">
380	        <p class="hero-caption-title" id="caption-title"></p>
381	        <time class="hero-caption-date" id="caption-date"></time>
382	      </figcaption>
383	    </div>
384	
385	    <div class="usage-row">
386	      <p class="usage-item"><span>New day, new photo, same URL</span> <code class="usage-url">https://api.nasapicture.com<button class="copy-btn" data-copy="https://api.nasapicture.com/" aria-label="Copy URL"><svg class="copy" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2"/><path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"/></svg><svg class="check" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg></button></code></p>
387	      <p class="usage-item"><span>Use it anywhere you'd put an image URL</span> <code class="usage-url">&lt;img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/"&gt;<button class="copy-btn" data-copy='&lt;img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/"&gt;' aria-label="Copy img tag"><svg class="copy" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2"/><path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"/></svg><svg class="check" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg></button></code></p>
388	    </div>

391	  <section class="info">
392	    <div class="fade-up">
393	      <h2>Endpoints</h2>
394	      <nav class="endpoint-list" aria-label="API endpoints">
395	        <a class="endpoint-row" href="https://api.nasapicture.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
396	          <span class="endpoint-route">/</span>
397	          <span class="endpoint-desc">HD image, falls back to standard</span>
398	          <span class="endpoint-type">image/*</span>
399	        </a>
400	        <a class="endpoint-row" href="https://api.nasapicture.com/sd" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
401	          <span class="endpoint-route">/sd</span>
402	          <span class="endpoint-desc">Standard resolution image</span>
403	          <span class="endpoint-type">image/*</span>
404	        </a>
405	        <a class="endpoint-row" href="https://api.nasapicture.com/info" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
406	          <span class="endpoint-route">/info</span>
407	          <span class="endpoint-desc">Title, date, explanation, URLs, copyright</span>
408	          <span class="endpoint-type">application/json</span>
409	        </a>
410	        <a class="endpoint-row" href="https://api.nasapicture.com/about" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
411	          <span class="endpoint-route">/about</span>
412	          <span class="endpoint-desc">Description and route listing</span>
413	          <span class="endpoint-type">text/plain</span>
414	        </a>
415	      </nav>
416	    </div>
417	  </section>
418	
419	  <footer>
420	    <div class="footer-inner">
421	      <p>Built by <a href="https://grantkeller.dev" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Grant Keller</a></p>
422	      <p>
423	        <a href="https://apod.nasa.gov/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">NASA APOD</a>
424	        <span class="footer-sep">/</span>
425	        <a href="https://github.com/GDKeller/nasa-picture-proxy" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Source</a>
426	      </p>
427	    </div>
428	  </footer>
429	
430	  <script>
431	    const API_BASE = 'http://localhost:8787';
432	    const img = document.getElementById('hero-img');
433	
434	    function onImageReady() { img.classList.add('loaded'); }
435	
436	    // Fetch APOD metadata, then set image + caption from live data
437	    fetch(`${API_BASE}/info`)
438	      .then(r => r.json())
439	      .then(data => {
440	        if (data.title) {
441	          img.src = data.hdurl || data.url;
442	          img.alt = data.title;
443	          document.getElementById('caption-title').textContent = data.title;
444	          document.getElementById('caption-date').textContent = data.date;
445	          document.getElementById('hero-caption').classList.add('visible');
446	        }
447	      })
448	      .catch(() => {});
449	
450	    img.addEventListener('load', onImageReady);
451	
452	    // Copy buttons
453	    document.querySelectorAll('.copy-btn').forEach(btn => {
454	      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
455	        const text = btn.dataset.copy;
456	        navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
457	          btn.classList.add('copied');
458	          setTimeout(() => btn.classList.remove('copied'), 2000);
459	        });
460	      });
461	    });
462	
463	    // Scroll fade-in
464	    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
465	      entries.forEach(entry => {
466	        if (entry.isIntersecting) {
467	          entry.target.classList.add('visible');
468	          observer.unobserve(entry.target);
469	        }
470	      });
471	    }, { threshold: 0.15 });
472	
473	    document.querySelectorAll('.fade-up').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));
474	  </script>
475	</body>
476	</html>
477
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 02:14:41Z)**_

Issues I found:

1. **`<figcaption>` outside `<figure>`** — invalid; must be a child of `<figure>`
2. **No `<main>` landmark** — required for accessibility
3. **`src=""` on `<img>`** — causes browser to request the current page URL; need a placeholder
4. **`<time>` missing `datetime` attribute** — needs to be set by JS
5. **Decorative SVGs lack `aria-hidden="true"`**
6. **Decorative separator `<span class="footer-sep">` needs `aria-hidden="true"`**

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 02:20:42Z)**_

Clean pass. Here's a summary of all the fixes:

- **Wrapped `<img>` + `<figcaption>` in `<figure>`** — `figcaption` requires a `figure` parent
- **Added `<main>` landmark** — wraps hero + info sections for screen reader navigation
- **Replaced empty `src=""` with transparent 1px GIF data URI** — prevents spurious request to current page
- **Added `datetime` attribute to `<time>`** — set by JS alongside the text content
- **Added `aria-hidden="true"` to decorative SVGs** — buttons already have `aria-label`
- **Added `aria-hidden="true"` to footer separator**
- **Added `type="button"` to copy buttons** — prevents implicit submit type

+---
+name: Next session QA plan
+description: QA the Cache API and dev logging changes before deploying to production
+type: project
+---
+
+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally
+2. **No date param** for "current" APOD requests — NASA handles "today" timezone logic
+3. **Per-request log buffer** — `log[]` array threaded through functions instead of module-level `logBuffer`
+4. **Dev logging format** — horizontal rule separator, then request summary, then indented cache lines, then wrangler's own line
+
+**Why:** Previous in-memory cache was wiped on every wrangler reload, burning through DEMO_KEY rate limits (30 req/hr). The UTC date bug caused 404s from NASA after midnight UTC but before midnight Eastern.
+
+**How to apply:** Test all routes (`/`, `/sd`, `/info`, `/about`) — verify cache HITs on second request, verify logs are clean, then deploy.
+
+```

+https://github.com/GDKeller/nasa-picture-proxy

 # NASA Picture Proxy
 
 A Cloudflare Worker that proxies [NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day](https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html) behind a fixed URL. An HTTP GET request returns today's image without needing an API key or parsing JSON.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 02:25:09Z)**_

Not complicated at all. The `API_BASE` already handles the fetch call. The remaining hardcoded `https://api.nasapicture.com` instances are:

1. **Header nav** `/about` link (line 365)
2. **Usage row** — display text + `data-copy` attributes (2 spots, line 387-388)
3. **Endpoint list** — 4 `href` attributes (lines 395-414)

That's 7 occurrences. You could add a few lines to the existing `<script>` that derive all URLs from `API_BASE`:

```js
document.querySelectorAll('a[href^="https://api.nasapicture.com"]').forEach(a => {
  a.href = a.href.replace('https://api.nasapicture.com', API_BASE);
});
```

Plus update the display text and `data-copy` values similarly. ~10 lines total. Want me to do it?

+```md
+---
+name: Next session QA plan
+description: QA the Cache API and dev logging changes before deploying to production
+type: project
+---
+
+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally
+2. **No date param** for "current" APOD requests — NASA handles "today" timezone logic
+3. **Per-request log buffer** — `log[]` array threaded through functions instead of module-level `logBuffer`
+4. **Dev logging format** — horizontal rule separator, then request summary, then indented cache lines, then wrangler's own line
+
+**Why:** Previous in-memory cache was wiped on every wrangler reload, burning through DEMO_KEY rate limits (30 req/hr). The UTC date bug caused 404s from NASA after midnight UTC but before midnight Eastern.
+
+**How to apply:** Test all routes (`/`, `/sd`, `/info`, `/about`) — verify cache HITs on second request, verify logs are clean, then deploy.
+
+```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 02:32:57Z)**_



A one-liner from the `landing/` directory:

```
npx serve landing
```

Or without any dependency:

```
python3 -m http.server 3000 -d landing
```

Either gives you `http://localhost:3000` (or 5000 for `serve`). The python option needs zero install.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 02:35:51Z)**_

That's doable — we could serve it from the Worker itself. Two options:

1. **Wrangler `assets` config** — add `assets = { directory = "landing" }` to `wrangler.toml` and it serves static files automatically, with the Worker handling API routes
2. **Inline the HTML** — import the HTML file in the Worker and serve it on a route like `/landing`

Option 1 is cleaner and how Cloudflare intends static assets to work. Want me to check your `wrangler.toml` and set it up?

### Configure Static Asset Directory in Wrangler

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/sites/start-from-worker

Specifies the local directory containing static assets that Wrangler will upload to Cloudflare KV. This configuration can be added to either wrangler.jsonc or wrangler.toml.

```jsonc
{
  "site": {
    "bucket": "./public" // Add the directory with your static assets!
  }
}
```

```toml
[site]
bucket = "./public"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Static Assets with No HTML Handling

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/routing/advanced/html-handling

This snippet demonstrates how to set a compatibility date and configure the [assets] block in wrangler.toml. By setting html_handling to 'none', the worker will only serve assets that match the request path exactly, without automatic .html or index resolution.

```toml
compatibility_date = "2026-03-25"

[assets]
directory = "./dist/"
html_handling = "none"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Static Assets in Wrangler

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets

Define the assets directory and binding in the Wrangler configuration file to enable static file hosting.

```wrangler.jsonc
{
  "$schema": "./node_modules/wrangler/config-schema.json",
  "name": "my-spa",
  "main": "src/index.js",
  // Set this to today's date
  "compatibility_date": "2026-03-25",
  "assets": {
    "directory": "./dist",
    "binding": "ASSETS"
  }
}
```

```wrangler.toml
"$schema" = "./node_modules/wrangler/config-schema.json"
name = "my-spa"
main = "src/index.js"
# Set this to today's date
compatibility_date = "2026-03-25"


[assets]
directory = "./dist"
binding = "ASSETS"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure SPA Assets in wrangler.toml

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/routing/single-page-application

Defines the configuration for serving static assets in a Single Page Application. It specifies the asset directory, sets the not_found_handling to 'single-page-application', and configures routing rules to run the Worker first for specific API paths.

```toml
compatibility_date = "2026-03-25"
main = "./src/index.ts"

[assets]
directory = "./dist/"
not_found_handling = "single-page-application"
binding = "ASSETS"
run_worker_first = [ "/api/*", "!/api/docs/*" ]
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Cloudflare Worker `wrangler.toml` for Assets and Routing

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets

This configuration file (`wrangler.toml`) defines the core settings for a Cloudflare Worker project. It specifies the `compatibility_date` for feature enablement, the `main` entry point for the Worker script, and detailed `assets` configuration. The `assets` section includes the `directory` for static files, `not_found_handling` for SPAs, the `binding` name for accessing assets, and `run_worker_first` rules to prioritize Worker execution for specific routes while excluding others.

```toml
# Set this to today's date
compatibility_date = "2026-03-25"
main = "./src/index.ts"


[assets]
directory = "./dist/"
not_found_handling = "single-page-application"
binding = "ASSETS"
run_worker_first = [ "/api/*", "!/api/docs/*" ]
```

--------------------------------

### Configure static site bucket in Wrangler

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/sites/start-from-existing

Defines the directory containing the static assets to be uploaded to Cloudflare KV. This configuration is required in either wrangler.jsonc or wrangler.toml.

```jsonc
{
  "site": {
    "bucket": "./public" // <-- Add your build directory name here.
  }
}
```

```toml
[site]
bucket = "./public"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Static Assets in Wrangler

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/wrangler/configuration

This example demonstrates how to configure static asset serving for your Cloudflare Worker. It includes specifying the directory containing your static files, an optional binding name for assets, and handling options for HTML content and 404 errors.

```json
{
  "assets": {
    "directory": "./public",
    "binding": "ASSETS",
    "html_handling": "force-trailing-slash",
    "not_found_handling": "404-page"
  }
}
```

```toml
[assets]
directory = "./public"
binding = "ASSETS"
html_handling = "force-trailing-slash"
not_found_handling = "404-page"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Static Asset Handling in Wrangler

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/vite-plugin/reference/static-assets

This configuration example demonstrates how to set up `not_found_handling` for single-page applications in your Cloudflare Worker project, ensuring that the root `index.html` serves as the fallback. Examples are provided for both `wrangler.jsonc` and `wrangler.toml`.

```jsonc
{
  "assets": {
    "not_found_handling": "single-page-application"
  }
}
```

```toml
[assets]
not_found_handling = "single-page-application"
```

--------------------------------

### Wrangler Configuration for Static Assets

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/routing/static-site-generation

Configures the Wrangler file to serve static assets from a specified directory, enables custom 404 page handling, and sets the HTML trailing slash behavior. This configuration is necessary for deploying SSG applications to Cloudflare Workers.

```jsonc
{
  "name": "my-worker",
  // Set this to today's date
  "compatibility_date": "2026-03-25",
  "assets": {
    "directory": "./dist/",
    "not_found_handling": "404-page",
    "html_handling": "auto-trailing-slash"
  }
}
```

```toml
name = "my-worker"
# Set this to today's date
compatibility_date = "2026-03-
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Wrangler for static Docusaurus assets

Source: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/framework-guides/web-apps/more-web-frameworks/docusaurus

Defines the configuration for serving static Docusaurus assets. The assets.directory field points to the production build folder, typically ./build.

```wrangler.jsonc
{
  "name": "my-docusaurus-app",
  "compatibility_date": "2026-03-25",
  "assets": {
    "directory": "./build"
  }
}
```

```wrangler.toml
name = "my-docusaurus-app"
compatibility_date = "2026-03-25"

[assets]
directory = "./build"
```

Here's the setup:

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `npm run dev` | Starts both servers backgrounded, logs to `logs/` |
| `npm run api` | Wrangler only (foreground) |
| `npm run dev:landing` | Landing only (foreground) |
| `npm run log` | `tail -f` both log files in real time |
| `npm run log:tail` | Last 20 lines of both logs |
| `npm run stop` | Kills both servers by port |

# Board Update

Analyze the codebase and git history to automatically reconcile the project board with reality. The system spec is bundled with the plugin at `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`.

## Board Data

Pre-loaded active board data:

filepath	type	status	priority	blocked_by	parent	title
./board/tasks/TASK-0001_build-landing-page.md	task	todo	-	-	NOTE-0002	Build landing page HTML
./board/tasks/TASK-0002_deploy-landing-page.md	task	todo	-	-	NOTE-0002	Deploy landing page to Cloudflare Pages
./board/tasks/TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md	task	todo	-	-	-	Apply for NASA Data Insignia
./board/planning/NOTE-0001_domain-strategy.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Domain strategy for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0002_landing-page.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0003_landing-page-critique.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page design critique (gallery revision)

Archive counts:

filepath	type	status	priority	blocked_by	parent	title
./board/tasks/TASK-0001_build-landing-page.md	task	todo	-	-	NOTE-0002	Build landing page HTML
./board/tasks/TASK-0002_deploy-landing-page.md	task	todo	-	-	NOTE-0002	Deploy landing page to Cloudflare Pages
./board/tasks/TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md	task	todo	-	-	-	Apply for NASA Data Insignia
./board/planning/NOTE-0001_domain-strategy.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Domain strategy for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0002_landing-page.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0003_landing-page-critique.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page design critique (gallery revision)

# Archive counts (type	count)

## Steps

### 1. Review Board State

Parse the pre-loaded board data above. Identify:
- All items with `status: backlog` or `status: current` (candidates for completion)
- Epics and their child tasks (via `parent` field)
- Blocked items and their blockers
- Items with terminal status (`done`, `processed`) that should be archived

### 2. Gather Codebase Context

- Run `git log --oneline` for recent commits (last 30) to understand what work has been done
- Only Read full file bodies for tasks whose status is `backlog` or `current` AND whose title suggests overlap with recent commits
- Use the context budget escalation (Glob → Grep → Read with limit → Full read) for any additional lookups

### 3. Detect Completed Work

For each task with `status: backlog` or `status: current`, check if the work described has already been done:

- Look at recent commits for evidence the work was completed
- Check the codebase for the feature/fix the task describes
- If the work is done, flag it for status update to `done`

### 4. Detect New Work Needed

Scan for things that should become tasks but aren't tracked yet:

- Check recent commits for TODOs or FIXMEs introduced in code
- Look at open epics — are there obvious next steps not yet captured as tasks?
- Check if any `blocked_by` dependencies have been resolved (unblocking tasks)

### 5. Update Epic Progress

For each active epic:

- Check if all child tasks are `done` — if so, flag the epic as `done`
- Check if any child tasks are `current` — if so, the epic should be `current`
- Update the task list in the epic body if tasks have been added/removed

### 6. Check for Stale Items

- Tasks marked `current` but with no related commits in the last 30 days
- Epics in `planning` with no child tasks
- Resources stuck in `inbox` or `processing`

### 7. Present Changes

Present ALL proposed changes as a single summary before making any edits:

```
## Board Update Proposal

### Status Changes
- TASK-0003: backlog -> done (cache-decoded-image: implemented in commit a1b2c3d)
- EPIC-0001: planning -> current (has active child tasks)

### New Tasks Suggested
- "Fix FIXME in preprocessing.rs line 42" (found in recent commit)

### Ready to Archive
- TASK-0003: done
- NOTE-0002: processed

### Stale Items
- TASK-0007: marked current but no activity in 45 days

### Unblocked
- TASK-0005: blocker TASK-0003 is now done
```

**Avoid redundant invocations** — if this skill was already invoked earlier in the session and the board has not been modified since, reuse the previous board data instead of invoking again.

Ask the user to approve, reject, or cherry-pick which changes to apply. Then execute only the approved changes:

- **Status updates**: Use `/board:board-edit` to update status fields
- **New tasks**: Invoke `/board:board-add` for each approved new task
- **Archiving**: Run `bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/archive-batch.sh ./board <file1> <file2> ...` directly for approved items

 `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`

```text
1	# Board System Specification
2	
3	> **Status:** Active
4	
5	A documentation and project management system designed for human-AI collaboration. Plain text files, minimal process, maximum clarity.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Guiding Principles
10	
11	These axioms inform every design decision in the system.
12	
13	### 1. Never Rely on Discipline
14	
15	Human willpower, memory, and routine are points of failure. They will fail — it's a matter of when, not if. The system must be designed so that:
16	- Nothing falls through the cracks due to forgotten reviews
17	- No periodic "check on X" rituals are required
18	- Relevant context surfaces automatically when needed
19	
20	### 2. Explicit Over Implicit
21	
22	Every outcome must be resolved explicitly. No vague items lingering in documents.
23	- ADRs must list spawned items or state "no follow-up needed"
24	- TASKs are actionable or they're not TASKs yet
25	- If something is worth tracking, decide what type it is
26	
27	### 3. Write-Once Artifacts
28	
29	Documents should be written once and not require ongoing maintenance.
30	- ADRs are immutable historical records
31	- Status reflects validity, not workflow
32	- No "update this when X happens" requirements
33	
34	### 4. One Home for Each Concern
35	
36	Every type of information has exactly one place:
37	- Work to do → TASK
38	- Decisions made → ADR
39	- Domain knowledge → REFE
40	- Unrefined thoughts → NOTE
41	- External material → RESO
42	
43	No overlap. No ambiguity about where something belongs.
44	
45	### 5. Binary Actionability
46	
47	Something is either actionable or it's not. No intermediate states.
48	- If you can write the first concrete step → TASK
49	- If it's still fuzzy → NOTE (refine until it's a TASK, or discard)
50	- No "maybe someday" purgatory
51	
52	### 6. Simple Over Complex
53	
54	Resist the pull toward JIRA/PM complexity hell.
55	- Minimal status values (3 for TASKs, 3 for ADRs)
56	- No custom fields proliferation
57	- If a workflow feels heavy, question whether it's necessary
58	
59	### 7. The Artifact is Separate from the Work
60	
61	- TASK = the work to be done
62	- ADR = the artifact produced by decision work
63	- REFE = the artifact produced by knowledge extraction
64	- Don't conflate process with output
65	
66	## Design Goals
67	
68	1. **Single source of truth** — metadata lives with the document, no separate index to maintain
69	2. **Self-identifying files** — type and ID visible in filename
70	3. **Standalone files** — each file makes sense without the organizational system
71	4. **Query-friendly** — structured metadata enables filtering and search
72	5. **Portable** — plain text (markdown + YAML) works anywhere
73	
74	---
75	
76	## Document Types
77	
78	### Overview
79	
80	| Type | Purpose | Status Values |
```
</details></tool-use>

+```md
+---
+name: Next session QA plan
+description: QA the Cache API and dev logging changes before deploying to production
+type: project
+---
+
+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally
+2. **No date param** for "current" APOD requests — NASA handles "today" timezone logic
+3. **Per-request log buffer** — `log[]` array threaded through functions instead of module-level `logBuffer`
+4. **Dev logging format** — horizontal rule separator, then request summary, then indented cache lines, then wrangler's own line
+
+**Why:** Previous in-memory cache was wiped on every wrangler reload, burning through DEMO_KEY rate limits (30 req/hr). The UTC date bug caused 404s from NASA after midnight UTC but before midnight Eastern.
+
+**How to apply:** Test all routes (`/`, `/sd`, `/info`, `/about`) — verify cache HITs on second request, verify logs are clean, then deploy.
+
+```

_**User (2026-03-30 08:57:26Z)**_

ok its initializing. here's a question. `https://api.nasapicture.com` doesn't end in an image file extension. will that be an issue for some people? would also making it available at api.nasapicture.com/image.png and api.nasapicture.com/image.jpg be possible and useful?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 08:57:35Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 08:57:37Z)**_

Most contexts (`<img>` tags, browsers, CSS) work fine without an extension — they use the `Content-Type` header, which the Worker already sets correctly.

But some places do care about the extension:
- Discord/Slack link previews sometimes won't embed without one
- Forum software that auto-detects images by URL pattern
- Some CMSes that validate image URLs by extension

It's trivial to add — just a pathname check in the Worker. The image would be whatever format NASA provides (usually JPEG) regardless of the extension. Want me to add those routes?

---

+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally
+2. **No date param** for "current" APOD requests — NASA handles "today" timezone logic
+3. **Per-request log buffer** — `log[]` array threaded through functions instead of module-level `logBuffer`

_**User (2026-03-30 09:19:24Z)**_

what was the issue?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 09:19:29Z)**_

The first deploy didn't specify `--branch main`, so it defaulted to the current git branch (`dev`), which Cloudflare treated as a **Preview** deployment. Only deployments on the production branch (`main`) get served on the custom domain. The second deploy with `--branch main` fixed it.

---
